data.name: TR-PICUS-TIER1-SCHEDULED_TASK_POWERSHELL: PowerShell Register-ScheduledTask Persistence Creation
data.description: CRITICAL PERSISTENCE DETECTION: Detects scheduled task creation via PowerShell cmdlets, a primary persistence mechanism observed in 18+ undetected Picus instances. Monitors for PowerShell Register-ScheduledTask, New-ScheduledTask, New-ScheduledTaskAction, and New-ScheduledTaskTrigger cmdlet execution. Unlike schtasks.exe (which is already detected), PowerShell-based task creation often evades detection because alerts focus on the binary, not the cmdlet. Observable from detonation: powershell.exe with Register-ScheduledTask parameters, often from non-administrator context. Critical for post-exploitation persistence.
data.contents: (process.name contains 'powershell.exe' OR process.name contains 'pwsh.exe') AND (process.command_line contains 'Register-ScheduledTask' OR process.command_line contains 'New-ScheduledTask' OR process.command_line contains 'New-ScheduledTaskAction' OR process.command_line contains 'New-ScheduledTaskTrigger' OR process.command_line contains 'New-ScheduledTaskPrincipal' OR process.command_line contains 'Set-ScheduledTask') AND (process.parent.name != 'explorer.exe' AND process.parent.name != 'svchost.exe')
